posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked: 1978 Chateau Laroque, Saint Emilion. Have a couple of these in the cellar so figured it was probably time to pop one open and see what’s going on. I have to say…I’m pretty impressed! Old school Bordeaux with red fruits, tobacco, smoke, and a structured/tannic finish. Medium bodied with plenty of acidity which has kept the wine really fresh. Well kept bottles should age and possibly integrate further with addiitonal age - I’ll probably look to open my last one in 3 to 5 years.

posts.name: P@trick Lynch
posts.cooked: Those old labels really make me swoon.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
J_Patrick_Lynch:

Those old labels really make me swoon

Gotta love the off-centered 1978 stamp!

posts.name: J0$hua Levin$0n
posts.cooked:
This gets me very excited about the few bottles of the recent (2018-present) Laroque I have been buying. I know styles, winemaking, even weather are different but love see the longevity from the producer.

Shows you don’t need to spend big to get truly age-worthy wines in Bordeaux.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
J.Levinson:

Shows you don’t need to spend big to get truly age-worthy wines in Bordeaux.

So true! Plenty of great values in Bordeaux…especially thanks to the legendary 2019 Costco deals as of late, ha!

posts.cooked:
Rich_Brown:

Well kept bottles should age and possibly integrate further with addiitonal age - I’ll probably look to open my last one in 3 to 5 years.

I’ll have a chance to communicate what a passively stored '75 tastes like if I get around to cracking the one my dad gave me. His basement isn’t super cold and I know nothing about this wine, so I’m not expecting a miracle.

posts.name: Ⓑⓞⓑⓑⓨ Beachy
posts.cooked: Did you decant for sediment and follow over time, or something else?

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
B_Beachy:

Did you decant for sediment and follow over time, or something else?

That’s exactly what i did. Had it upright for about a week, decanted for sentiment and drank right away. Still have a glass left for tonight in a qik Vin preservation decanter so we’ll see how that’s doing tonight

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Had a 2001 blind on Friday night. Two people said Ausone. It remained super youthful and the palate was great, though the élevage-derived lactones were a distraction for me on the nose.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

Had a 2001 blind on Friday night. Two people said Ausone. It remained super youthful and the palate was great, though the élevage-derived lactones were a distraction for me on the nose.

Wow, that’s pretty high praise, especially considering the cost of these wines!

And side note. I wish my palate was anywhere close to being sharp enough to call out that élevage-derived lactones were a distraction for me during a blind tasting, ha! Cheers bud!

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Just a somewhat pompous way of saying the oak was too creamy
